title: iPhone Fold launching soon: Expected price, display, camera, colours and other specifications
summary: The iPhone Fold is expected to launch in September alongside the iPhone 18 Pro, with a price range of $2,000 to $2,500. It features a foldable design, A20 Pro chipset, and quad camera setup, while lacking FaceID and supporting only eSIM.

title: Are your apps secretly stealing your data? FBI warns iPhone and Android users over Chinese mobile apps
summary: The FBI has warned of risks linked to downloading mobile apps, particularly those developed in China. The agency says such apps could expose user data to the Chinese government, raising privacy concerns.

title: Claude AI can now search your Outlook emails and Teams chats for free: Here's how it works
summary: Anthropic has enabled all Claude users to access the Microsoft 365 connector. The feature was previously available only to the company's Team and Enterprise users. With the new integration users can search and analyze data from Microsoft apps without manual uploading the files.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's Meta builds new AI hardware divison, hires hires veteran engineer for mysterious AI gadget: Report
summary: Meta is assembling a hardware team under its Superintelligence Labs and is hiring Rui Xu from Dreamer to lead the effort. The move is said to indicate the company's interest in developing AI gadgets beyond beyond smart glasses and VR headsets.

title: SpaceX lays out IPO details, targets early June roadshow
summary: SpaceX outlined details of its highly anticipated IPO at a meeting with its team of bankers Monday night, telling them it plans to earmark a large portion of shares for retail investors and will host 1,500 of them at an event in June following the IPO ‌roadshow launch.

title: Nvidia acquisition of SchedMD sparks worry among AI specialists about software access
summary: Nvidia last December announced it would acquire SchedMD, giving it control ​of open-source software called Slurm, which schedules computing tasks and is critical for training large language models that power chatbots such as Anthropic's Claude. title: Anthropic tops $30 billion run rate, seals deal with Broadcom
summary: Anthropic PBC said its revenue run rate has now topped $30 billion, up from $9 billion at the end of 2025, and confirmed plans to work with Broadcom and Google to power its burgeoning operations.

title: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google unite to combat model copying in China
summary: Rivals OpenAI, Anthropic PBC, and Alphabet’s Google have begun working together to try to clamp down on Chinese competitors extracting results from cutting-edge US artificial intelligence models to gain an edge in the global AI race. title: Netflix debuts new Playground gaming app for kids
summary: Netflix doubled down on its gaming ​efforts on Monday, launching ​a new app called "Netflix Playground" that would feature games ​built around popular children's characters such as Peppa Pig and Sesame Street.

title: ET Graphics: Future of search is on fast track
summary: ​​A new realm is now developing alongside it. Generative engine optimisation (GEO) is about being the source that AI tools like ChatGPT, Perplexity and Google’s AI Overviews draw from when they answer a question. Not a link in a list, the answer itself.

title: AI’s defence push raises alarm bells at top enterprises
summary: Talking Point Firms are pausing AI projects amid rising fears around data exposure, governance gaps, geopolitical tensions & dependence on global providers, ET writes.

summary: A cross-section of companies may challenge AI platforms over the diversion of users away from their websites and excluding them from AI-generated responses without explanation or recourse, writes ET.
